notes.note: Suessa Aurunca, a town in Latium Adiectum (modern Sessa Aurunca, Campania), was a Roman municipium whose citizens held full Roman citizenship.

notes.anchor: In the Cretan war, under the command of Metellus
notes.note: The Cretan War (69–67 BC), fought by Q. Caecilius Metellus Creticus, who was awarded the cognomen Creticus for subduing the island. Nasennius's service here dates his military career to roughly a generation before 43 BC.
"Octavum principem" designates the leading soldier (princeps) of the eighth maniple or century — a position of non-commissioned seniority in the hastati or principes line of a Republican legion.

Cicero asks Brutus (commanding in Macedonia) to give Nasennius a military post or honorific attachment to his staff; such commendations from Cicero to commanders were a standard form of political patronage.
